narrative:
We're stuck with another couple of days of unsettled weather. There will be some dry time, but occasional showers and thunderstorms are going to be moving through, too. We can't rule out locally heavy rainfall and gusty winds in any stronger storms that pop up this afternoon. Clouds and rain will keep temperatures down, but drier weather returns for Tuesday along with the start of a warming trend.

2027 Blue Ridge Calendar

The 2027 Blue Ridge Calendar is here and being delivered to our retail outlets. Thanks for a record-breaking pre-publication sale! You can still avoid shipping costs with our pickup events beginning Thursday, July 2, in Boone. We'll take that to Banner Elk, West Jefferson, Spruce Pine, and Black Mountain next week; click here for details. You can see all the winning photography at our Photo Contest page . Check out volume discounts on the calendar and the "Mix and Match" sale on Drinkware.

Showery Sunday

Last Updated Sun July 12 at 9:27AM by Alan Simmons

We're stuck with another couple of days of unsettled weather. There will be some dry time, but occasional showers and thunderstorms are going to be moving through, too. We can't rule out locally heavy rainfall and gusty winds in any stronger storms that pop up this afternoon. Clouds and rain will keep temperatures down, but drier weather returns for Tuesday along with the start of a warming trend.

Forecast Discussion

Weak low pressure meanders around the Tennessee Valley the next couple of days, bringing areas of showers and thunderstorms that will roll through from time to time. There will be some drier spells, but keep an eye to the sky and have a backup plan ready. There's plenty of moisture and instability for a few local downpours and strong, gusty winds with the more active cells this afternoon. The low moves away and weakens further by Tuesday, allowing weak high pressure over the Great Lakes region to push south toward our area and nicer weather to return. Temperatures will start to ramp up, and the second half of the week is even warmer as the high sets up shop over the Gulf Coast. Along with the warmer temperatures, the return of more humidity will give us a daily cycle of widely separated afternoon showers and thundershowers that take us into next weekend.

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Monday Message for July 6, 2026

It's the first Monday Message of July! Mercifully, temperatures are cooler this week, although rainfall continues to be scattered and somewhat unreliable. We also take a look back at June rainfall and temperatures.

Monday Message for June 29, 2026

It's the Independence Day / America 250 edition of the Monday Message, available to subscribers. This week, it's all about heat, humidity and isolated afternoon thunderstorms.
